name: Chocolate Upside Down Apricot Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. In a 9-inch square baking pan, melt 2 tablespoons butter in the oven.
Remove from the oven and stir in brown sugar.
3. Arrange apricot halves with the rounded side down and cherries in the pan.
4. In a small mixing bowl, beat together 1/3 cup butter and granulated sugar until light and fluffy.
5. Add cocoa and cinnamon, beating until well blended.
6. Stir in vanilla. Pour batter into the pan over the fruit.
7. Bake for 45 to 50 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
8. Using a metal spatula, loosen the sides and invert the cake onto a serving plate.
9. Serve warm.
NOTE: I didn't want a square cake, so I baked this in a 9-cup Bundt pan, well sprayed with Baker's Joy. It took about 40 minutes and was very full - could have used a 12 cup pan. With the apricot halves arranged in the large depressions and slices in the small ones, along with cherries here and there, it's very beautiful, most unusual, and tastes wonderful. From the 1934 Hershey's Cookbook: JOANN MILLER (TXRM93A)
